free_text: New measurements and evaluation of excitation
reactions on 133Cs up to 70 MeV proton energy
CsCl targets (80-90 mg/cm2) pressed into high purity
aluminum cup (478 micron bottom thickness) and
covered with 12 micron Ti monitor foils in case of
VUB. Self-supporting CsCl pellets (120 mg/cm2) were
with 100 micron Al backings and 10 micron Al front
CYRIC and with 100 micron Al backings, 50 micron Al
12 micron Ti monitor foils at the VUB irradiations.
To minimize the water content of the target, the
CsCl powder was dried under vacuum and the pellets
were sealed in plastic bags till irradiation. The
targets were irradiated in vacuum (VUB) or in helium
gas atmosphere (CYRIC). Irradiations were done with
proton beams for about half an hour. Charge measured
in Faraday-cup. Monitor reactions on Al and Ti were
used to correct the initial beam energy and
Decay data were taken from NuDat database.

by calibrated magnetic bending in Sendai and by

The experimental data were compared to the
